headline: Emmanuel Grégoire, an omnipresent Paris mayor with a centralized governance
subtitle: In his first few months, the new Socialist mayor has broken with Anne Hidalgo's remote style, micromanaging every portfolio. His grip is starting to test the patience of allies.

headline: Major fire rages in Fontainebleau forest near Paris
Yves Tramblay, hydrologist: 'Successive heatwaves are causing a very significant and rapid loss of water stored in soils'
'It would have been enough to learn the terrible lessons from Gaza to anticipate the current impasse over Iran'
subtitle: The deals that US President Donald Trump so readily boasts about seemed destined to get bogged down in the Middle East right from the start.

headline: 'If they catch me, it's over for me': Senegal's LGBTQ+ community lives in daily fear of repression
subtitle: In March, Senegal passed a law increasing penalties for same-sex relations, leaving LGBTQ+ people living in fear. Several described their deeply uncertain daily lives, gripped by the constant anxiety of being arrested.

headline: 'I would do a line before service, hidden in the bathroom': How cocaine is flooding the restaurant industry
subtitle: Cocaine at work (2/3). From fast-food joints to Michelin-starred restaurants, drug use has become as commonplace as it is alarming in an industry driven by the pursuit of performance, a toxic party culture and grueling working conditions.

headline: French heatwave drives up water consumption, puts supply networks under severe strain
subtitle: Water usage rose by 10% to 50% in June compared to the same period in 2025, forcing already fragile infrastructure to operate at full capacity, while underground reserves and rivers weakened.

'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subtitle: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

headline: 'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
subtitle: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.

headline: Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
subtitle: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.
